name: Hubbsina turneri
summary: The Hubbsina Turner’s fish, a stunning little fish found exclusively in the wetlands of Central America, is teetering on the brink of extinction.  Its fragile habitat—including freshwater lakes, rivers, and marshes—is threatened by increasing water usage, human activities like recreation, and the expansion of land development.  You can help protect this remarkable species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices.

name: Ameca splendens
summary: The Ameca splendens, a stunning fish found in the wetlands and rivers of Southeast Asia, is facing a dire crisis and is now critically endangered. Its survival is threatened by a combination of factors including dams, water diversion, and habitat destruction from agriculture and human activities. You can help protect this incredible species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable water management practices.

name: Neochanna burrowsius

description.en: Artificial/Aquatic - Canals and Drainage Channels, Ditches

summary: The Neochanna burrowsius, a tiny fish found in Australia’s wetlands and waterways, is now critically endangered and its numbers are sadly declining. These unique fish are threatened by activities like farming, water extraction, and habitat changes, which are drastically altering their delicate home environments. You can help protect the Neochanna burrowsius by supporting sustainable land management practices and advocating for the preservation of wetland ecosystems.

name: Allotoca diazi
summary: The Allotoca diazi, also known as the Desert Pupfish, is a tiny, incredibly rare fish found only in the unique wetlands of Mexico’s Chihuahuan Desert.  These remarkable creatures are threatened by pollution from sewage and agricultural runoff, as well as the draining of their vital freshwater habitats. You can help protect the Desert Pupfish by supporting organizations working to restore and conserve these fragile desert ecosystems.

name: Poblana alchichica
summary: The Poblana alchichica, a tiny fish found only in the freshwater lakes of central Mexico, is critically endangered and its numbers are sadly shrinking. These unique fish face serious threats from increasing tourism and the draining of groundwater used for drinking water, disrupting their delicate wetland homes.  You can help protect the Poblana alchichica by supporting sustainable tourism practices and advocating for responsible water management in the region.

name: Poblana ferdebueni
summary: The Poblana ferdebueni, a stunning fish found exclusively in the wetlands and rivers of central Mexico, is critically endangered and its numbers are sadly dwindling.  Threats to this unique species include unknown factors, combined with pollution from agricultural runoff, excessive water usage, and erosion, all of which are damaging its fragile habitat.  You can help protect the Poblana ferdebueni by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable water management practices in the region.

name: Poblana letholepis
summary: The Poblana letholepis, a stunning fish found in the wetlands of central Mexico, is on the brink of extinction.  Threats like soil erosion and the draining of its vital freshwater habitats due to farming and water usage are pushing this species towards a devastating unknown future.  You can help protect the Poblana letholepis by supporting organizations working to restore and conserve these precious wetland ecosystems.

name: Poblana squamata
summary: The Poblana squamata, a strikingly patterned fish, is critically endangered and its populations are sadly declining. Found exclusively in the unique wetlands and rivers of central Mexico, this fish faces unknown threats potentially exacerbated by dam construction and other unreported dangers. You can help protect this incredible species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices in Mexico.

name: Clarias batu
summary: The Clarias batu, a unique catfish, is critically endangered and struggling to survive in the permanent rivers and wetlands of southern Africa.  Its home is under serious threat from excessive water removal for farming and household use, compounded by increasing tourism and the impact of introduced fish species.  You can help protect this incredible fish by supporting sustainable water management practices and advocating for responsible tourism in its fragile habitats.

name: Oreoglanis lepturus
summary: The Oreoglanis lepturus, or Mexican cave catfish, is a remarkable fish found only in the unique wetlands of Mexico, particularly within permanent rivers and streams. Sadly, this critically endangered species is threatened by pollution from sewage and waste, impacts from mining and dam construction, and habitat disturbance.  You can help protect this amazing fish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and reducing your own plastic consumption to minimize pollution in our waterways.

name: Betta fusca
summary: The Betta fusca, a stunning red and black fish, is critically endangered and found only in the unique wetlands of Southeast Asia, particularly in permanent rivers, streams, and waterfalls. Sadly, its future is uncertain, and the primary threats facing this species remain largely unknown, likely including habitat loss and pollution. You can help protect the Betta fusca by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable practices in the region where it lives.

name: Hyalobagrus ornatus
summary: The Hyalobagrus ornatus, a stunning fish found in the wetlands of Southeast Asia, is now critically endangered and facing a serious decline. Primarily threatened by expanding farms, growing cities, and harmful chemicals entering its rivers and streams, this unique fish is disappearing. You can help protect the Hyalobagrus ornatus by supporting organizations working to reduce pollution and conserve these vital river habitats.
